message:
chore(beads): file two new redis parity bugs (84bv embstr promotion, ifuw ACL CAT gaps)

Adds two open bug entries surfaced by recent ad-hoc differential probes
of fr (port 16700) against vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701):

  frankenredis-84bv [P3 / bug]
    fr-store: APPEND/SETRANGE doesn't promote string encoding from
    embstr to raw.  Upstream Redis sticky-promotes mutated short strings
    to 'raw' once touched; fr-store derives encoding solely from current
    length, so a mutated short value still reports 'embstr' on
    OBJECT ENCODING.  Implementation lands in commit 1cf36e9 (this same
    branch) and references the same br id, so this entry is filed
    primarily for trace/audit and follow-up coverage.

  frankenredis-ifuw [P3 / bug]
    fr-command: ACL CAT category listings under-include relative to
    upstream redis 7.2.  ACL CAT slow returns 3 commands vs upstream's
    271 (upstream treats 'slow' as the inverse of the 'fast' flag, so
    nearly every command is implicitly 'slow' there).  ACL CAT keyspace,
    admin, and connection are also short; subcommand-form names like
    'client|info' / 'object|encoding' are missing entirely.  Fix needs to
    parse subcommand metadata from legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/
    *.json and broaden the 'slow' computation to use !fast.  No
    implementation in this commit -- bug entry only.

Note on the file as committed: this commit intentionally adds only the
two new entries; the full local beads.db -> JSONL flush that produced
the working-tree diff also contained a stale revert of frankenredis-ky4n
(closed in commit 203d870) back to open.  That revert was excluded from
this commit so the recent close stays in tree; the local beads.db drift
will be reconciled separately.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id": "frankenredis-7vqg", "title": "redis parity: CLUSTER SHARDS should return topology when cluster mode is enabled", "description": "Local cluster_cmd in crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs still hard-disables CLUSTER SHARDS after arity validation, even when store.cluster_enabled is true. Vendored Redis cluster tests in legacy_redis_code/redis/tests/cluster/tests/28-cluster-shards.tcl expect CLUSTER SHARDS to expose shard/node topology and to survive handshake edges. Add a Redis-compatible enabled path and reply shape for CLUSTER SHARDS.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-21T12:52:22.418064990Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-21T18:31:15.683844101Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-21T18:31:15.681924757Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-7zyl", "title": "redis parity: SCRIPT subcommands should reject script context", "description": "Vendored Redis marks SCRIPT LOAD, EXISTS, FLUSH, DEBUG, and KILL as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/script-load.json, script-exists.json, script-flush.json, script-debug.json, and script-kill.json. The local script_cmd implementation at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:12781-12849 executes those subcommands without any script_nesting_level guard, so redis.call('SCRIPT', ...) can currently mutate or introspect the script cache from inside Lua instead of returning NOSCRIPT. Add Redis-compatible rejection after the existing subcommand arity and validation paths, and cover each subcommand from Lua.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-21T07:03:38.274942976Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-21T15:54:12.879529740Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-21T15:54:12.877642600Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-80t", "title": "Wire CONFIG SET client-output-buffer-limit to fr-server", "description": "## Problem

fr-server/src/main.rs line 39 defines `const MAX_WRITE_BUFFER: usize = 1024 * 1024` (1 MiB). Redis has client-output-buffer-limit with separate thresholds for normal, replica, and pubsub clients (with both hard and soft limits plus timeout).

## What Redis Does

CONFIG SET client-output-buffer-limit has the form:
  client-output-buffer-limit <class> <hard limit> <soft limit> <soft seconds>

Classes: normal, replica (slave), pubsub. Default hard limits:
- normal: 0 (unlimited)
- replica: 256mb
- pubsub: 32mb

The current flat 1 MiB limit is far too restrictive for replica and pubsub clients.

## Implementation Plan

1. Define a struct for per-class output buffer limits (hard, soft, soft_seconds)
2. Add to ServerState with Redis defaults
3. Handle CONFIG SET client-output-buffer-limit parsing
4. In fr-server, check the appropriate class limit per-client instead of the flat const
5. For pubsub clients, use the pubsub limit; for replicas, use the replica limit

## Files to Modify

- `crates/fr-runtime/src/lib.rs`: Limit struct, CONFIG SET handler
- `crates/fr-server/src/main.rs`: Per-client class-based limit checking", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:04:47.858589736Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-03-25T03:59:29.660410811Z", "closed_at": "2026-03-25T03:59:29.659812268Z", "close_reason": "output_buffer_limit (256 MiB default) added to ServerState, fr-server reads runtime value, MAX_WRITE_BUFFER const removed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "frankenredis-80t", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-kgn", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-03-25T03:04:51.994434558Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
+{"id":"frankenredis-84bv","title":"fr-store: APPEND/SETRANGE doesn't promote string encoding from embstr to raw","description":"Upstream Redis 7.2 encodes short strings (≤44 bytes) as 'embstr' but irreversibly promotes them to 'raw' when modified by APPEND, SETRANGE, GETSET, or any other in-place mutation. fr-store's object_encoding at crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:2796 derives the encoding solely from the current length (≤44 → embstr, else raw), so a short value modified by APPEND still reports as 'embstr'.

Acceptance: 'SET k short; APPEND k more; OBJECT ENCODING k' returns 'raw' (matching vendored redis 7.2.4) instead of 'embstr'. Likewise SETRANGE on an embstr value should report 'raw' afterwards.

Reference: ad-hoc differential probe of fr (port 16700) vs vendored redis 7.2.4 (port 16701). After SET k 'short' + APPEND k '_more': fr 'embstr', redis 'raw'.

Fix sketch: add a  field to fr-store::Entry; mutating ops (append, setrange, getset that converts via copy) flip it true; object_encoding short-circuits to 'raw' when set.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T04:12:24.091114304Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T04:12:24.091114304Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-859d", "title": "Activate FR-P2C-006 replication journey in the live-oracle matrix", "description": "Evidence: crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/user_workflow_corpus_v1.json still marks the FR-P2C-006 differential_hook as planned, even though crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/fr_p2c_006_replication_journey.json already exists and is command-shaped for live Redis differential runs. That packet journey is also absent from crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/live_oracle_matrix.json.\n\nNeed: graduate the FR-P2C-006 replication differential hook from planned to active by wiring the journey fixture into the live-oracle matrix, adding the corresponding orchestrator/test entrypoint, and reconciling any expectation drift against upstream Redis for WAIT/WAITAOF/replication-marker behavior.\n\nReal impl path: crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/user_workflow_corpus_v1.json, crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/live_oracle_matrix.json, crates/fr-conformance/fixtures/fr_p2c_006_replication_journey.json, and the live oracle test/orchestrator code in crates/fr-conformance/src/lib.rs.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-22T18:20:06.187536106Z", "created_by": "cod-redis", "updated_at": "2026-04-22T21:01:21.347914998Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-22T21:01:21.347914998Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["conformance", "fr-conformance", "replication", "testing"]}

 {"id": "frankenredis-icsm", "title": "EPIC: Establish real performance baseline with redis-benchmark-compatible harness and p50/p95/p99 latency tracking", "description": "# Performance Evidence Epic

## Why This Is Critical
The README describes "Deterministic Latency Replication Core (DLRC)" as the "Crown-Jewel Innovation" and claims "profile-proven performance" with "p95/p99 latency tracking." Yet the repo contains ZERO throughput benchmarks, ZERO latency percentile measurements, and an empty baselines/ directory. The only checked-in artifacts measure conformance test execution speed (~1.3ms), not Redis workload performance.

This is the single most dangerous gap because performance is the stated reason this project exists as a differentiator from legacy Redis. Without evidence, DLRC is marketing copy, not engineering.

## What Success Looks Like
- A redis-benchmark-compatible harness that measures ops/sec, latency histograms (p50/p95/p99/p999), and memory usage under mixed workloads
- Baseline numbers checked into baselines/ with comparison against legacy Redis on identical workloads
- CI-gatable regression detection: any commit that degrades p99 by >10% fails the gate
- The README performance claims are either proven or honestly revised

## Architecture
The benchmark harness should be a new binary in fr-server or a dedicated crate that:
1. Connects via real TCP to a running frankenredis instance
2. Runs configurable workloads (SET/GET mixed, pipeline depth, key sizes)
3. Records latency histograms using HdrHistogram or equivalent
4. Outputs JSON artifacts compatible with the existing artifacts/ structure
5. Compares against baselines/ and reports deltas

## Dependencies
All sub-tasks feed into this epic. The benchmark harness must exist before any optimization work can be proven.", "status": "closed", "priority": 0, "issue_type": "epic", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:10:22.979891472Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-10T02:20:30.057699153Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-10T02:20:30.057479858Z", "close_reason": "All sub-tasks completed: benchmark harness (bxlo), baselines (gess), regression gate (1zxx) all closed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "dependencies": [{"issue_id": "frankenredis-icsm", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-1zxx", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:16.512824074Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-icsm", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-bxlo", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:16.348066219Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}, {"issue_id": "frankenredis-icsm", "depends_on_id": "frankenredis-gess", "type": "blocks", "created_at": "2026-04-07T03:11:16.429198141Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "metadata": "{}", "thread_id": ""}]}
 {"id": "frankenredis-ie3f", "title": "redis parity: CONFIG GET/SET/RESETSTAT/REWRITE should reject script context", "description": "Vendored Redis marks CONFIG GET, CONFIG SET, CONFIG RESETSTAT, and CONFIG REWRITE as NOSCRIPT in legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/config-get.json, config-set.json, config-resetstat.json, and config-rewrite.json, but config_cmd at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:10363 serves all four subcommands without any script_nesting_level guard. Add Redis-compatible rejection while preserving existing arity and subcommand validation order.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-21T04:07:16.387435544Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-21T04:13:26.624349627Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-21T04:13:26.622405788Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
+{"id":"frankenredis-ifuw","title":"fr-command: ACL CAT category listings missing many commands (slow has 3 vs upstream 271)","description":"fr-command::command_matches_acl_category at crates/fr-command/src/lib.rs:11051 has hardcoded category mappings that significantly under-include compared to upstream Redis 7.2 commands.def. Concrete gaps from a differential probe vs vendored redis 7.2.4:

- ACL CAT slow: fr returns 3 commands, upstream returns 271. Most commands have an implicit 'slow' category in upstream when the 'fast' flag isn't set; fr only returns commands explicitly flagged 'slow'.
- ACL CAT keyspace: fr 28, upstream 34 (missing dbsize, flushall, flushdb, migrate, restore-asking; subcommand-form names like object|encoding etc.)
- ACL CAT admin: fr 23, upstream 65
- ACL CAT connection: fr 8, upstream ~30 (missing all subcommand-form names like client|info, command|docs)

Acceptance: ACL CAT outputs match vendored redis 7.2.4 set semantics (order can differ — sorted comparison). Subcommand-form names ('cmd|sub') should be included for any command that has subcommands (CLIENT|, ACL|, COMMAND|, CLUSTER|, FUNCTION|, MEMORY|, OBJECT|, etc.).

Reference: legacy_redis_code/redis/src/commands/*.json — each has 'arguments' that include subcommands and 'acl_categories' for both the parent and subcommands. Fix: parse and emit subcommand-form names; broaden 'slow' to include ALL commands lacking the 'fast' flag (matches upstream's getCommandFlags inverse-fast logic).","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-30T04:11:06.251072333Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-30T04:11:06.251072333Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-ifvn", "title": "CONFORMANCE: redis migrate parity matrix", "description": "Wire core_migrate.json via live_redis_diff. MIGRATE DUMP/RESTORE/REPLACE/KEYS/COPY/AUTH.", "status": "closed", "priority": 1, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-24T12:34:57.223703267Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-24T22:55:02.758908819Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-24T22:55:02.757057644Z", "close_reason": "Live-oracle matrix test passing 100% under FR_CONFORMANCE_LIVE_ORACLE_STRICT=1.", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-ikmv", "title": "RDB stream decode should fail on truncated group header", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "bug", "created_at": "2026-04-13T18:12:50.092444493Z", "created_by": "ubuntu", "updated_at": "2026-04-13T18:12:52.521300377Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-13T18:12:52.521106940Z", "close_reason": "Decode now enforces stream group length; added truncated-stream regression test", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0}
 {"id": "frankenredis-iniy", "title": "Add bitfield and BITOP fuzz target for fr-store binary string mutations", "description": "Evidence: there is no direct fuzz target for fr-store bit mutation surfaces even though they perform offset arithmetic and dynamic buffer growth. The uncovered entry points are crates/fr-store/src/lib.rs:2290 (bitfield_get), 2320 (bitfield_set), 8000 (bitop), plus internal helpers 10206 (bitfield_read) and 10235 (bitfield_write). Existing fuzz_runtime_sequence does not drive these paths.\n\nNeed: add a structure-aware fuzz target that mutates offsets, signed/unsigned widths, BITOP opcodes, and source/destination key combinations, then checks invariants against a small shadow bit-vector model: reads after writes agree, no-op writes preserve digest, BITOP output length matches Redis rules, and hostile offsets never panic or corrupt unrelated keys.\n\nReal impl path: fuzz/Cargo.toml plus new fuzz/fuzz_targets/fuzz_store_bitops.rs calling the fr-store bitfield/bitop APIs directly.", "status": "closed", "priority": 2, "issue_type": "task", "created_at": "2026-04-22T17:48:14.984926124Z", "created_by": "cod-redis", "updated_at": "2026-04-22T19:34:01.841789756Z", "closed_at": "2026-04-22T19:34:01.839885261Z", "close_reason": "Completed", "source_repo": ".", "compaction_level": 0, "original_size": 0, "labels": ["fr-store", "fuzz", "testing"]}
